(When the parachute opens,) air resistance is greater than weight (So) resultant force is upwards and acceleration is upwards or (so) resultant force is upwards and the skydiver decelerates (As speed decreases) air resistance decreases (so acceleration/deceleration decreases) (Until upthrust +) air resistance = weight (so) resultant force = 0 and acceleration = 0 (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow drag for air resistance throughout Ignore speed decreases Ignore speed is constant (Total for Question 20 = 15 marks) TOTAL FOR PAPER = 80 MARKS Pearson Education Limited.
